title: Top 20 System Design Problems
description: The most frequently asked system design interview questions with structured approach guides. Each problem includes scale requirements, key components, and discussion points.

problems.title: Design a URL Shortener (bit.ly)
problems.approach: Start with API design (POST /shorten, GET /:code). Hash or counter-based ID generation. Key-value store for O(1) lookups. Cache hot URLs. Analytics via async event pipeline.

problems.title: Design Twitter/X Feed
problems.approach: Fan-out on write for regular users, fan-out on read for celebrities. ML-based ranking of candidates. Cache pre-computed timelines. WebSocket for real-time updates.

problems.title: Design a Chat System (WhatsApp/Slack)
problems.approach: WebSocket gateway fleet for persistent connections. Message service with per-conversation ordering. Presence via heartbeat + pub/sub. Fan-out for group messages. Offline delivery via push + catch-up sync.

problems.title: Design YouTube/Netflix Video Streaming
problems.approach: Upload: transcode to multiple resolutions/codecs asynchronously. Serve via CDN with adaptive bitrate streaming (DASH/HLS). Buffer-based ABR algorithm. Recommendation engine for homepage/next video.

problems.title: Design Google Search
problems.approach: Crawler builds web graph. Inverted index maps terms to document lists. Ranking combines relevance (BM25/TF-IDF), link analysis (PageRank), freshness, and user signals. Serve from distributed index shards.

problems.title: Design an E-commerce Platform (Amazon)
problems.approach: Microservices: catalog, cart, order, payment, inventory, shipping. Event-driven order processing with saga pattern. Inventory with optimistic locking. Search via Elasticsearch. Recommendation engine.

problems.title: Design a Ride-Sharing System (Uber)
problems.approach: H3/S2 geospatial indexing for driver locations. Real-time matching with optimization. Stream processing for dynamic pricing. ML-based ETA with live traffic overlay.

problems.title: Design a Rate Limiter
problems.approach: Token bucket or sliding window algorithm. Store per-client state in Redis with atomic operations (Lua scripts). Return rate limit headers. Handle distributed deployments with centralized or local + sync approach.

problems.title: Design a Notification System
problems.approach: API receives events, preference service filters, template engine renders, channel-specific workers deliver (push, email, SMS). Priority queues for urgency levels. Track delivery and engagement.

problems.title: Design a Distributed Cache (Redis)
problems.approach: Consistent hashing for key distribution across nodes. Primary-replica replication for availability. LRU/LFU eviction. Handle cache invalidation with TTL and explicit invalidation. Support data structures beyond key-value.

problems.title: Design a Web Crawler
problems.approach: Seed URLs -> priority frontier -> distributed fetchers with per-domain rate limiting -> parser extracts links -> dedup with bloom filter -> index content. Handle robots.txt, redirects, and dynamic pages.

problems.title: Design Dropbox/Google Drive
problems.approach: Client watches filesystem, chunks files (4MB blocks), deduplicates with content hashing, uploads changed chunks. Metadata service tracks file tree and versions. Sync protocol resolves conflicts (last-writer-wins or create copy). Block storage for chunks.

problems.title: Design a Key-Value Store (DynamoDB)
problems.approach: Consistent hashing for partition assignment. Paxos/Raft per partition for replication. Tunable consistency (strong via leader reads, eventual via any-replica reads). Handle partitioning, node failures, and data rebalancing.
problems.scaleTarget: Millions of operations/sec, petabytes of data

problems.title: Design a Metrics/Monitoring System (Datadog)
problems.approach: Agents collect metrics, send to ingestion service via UDP/HTTP. Store in time-series database with downsampling for older data. Pre-compute aggregations. Alerting engine evaluates rules against recent data. Dashboard queries with rollup functions.

problems.title: Design a Payment System (Stripe)
problems.approach: Idempotent API with idempotency keys. Event-sourced transaction log. Saga pattern for multi-step flows (auth, capture, settle). Async reconciliation with bank statements. ML-based fraud scoring. PCI-DSS compliance.

problems.title: Design a Social Graph (Facebook)
problems.approach: Adjacency list storage partitioned by user ID. Cache hot social graphs in memory. Friend-of-friend queries with BFS depth limit. Recommendation: collaborative filtering on graph structure. Privacy: per-edge visibility rules evaluated at query time.

problems.title: Design a Message Queue (Kafka)
problems.approach: Topics with partitions (ordered, append-only logs). Producer partitions by key hash. Consumer groups with partition assignment. Replication for durability. Offset-based consumption for replay capability.

problems.title: Design an Autocomplete/Typeahead System
problems.approach: Trie storing top-K completions at each node. Precompute suggestions offline from query logs. Serve from distributed cache with prefix-based lookup. Personalization layer adds user history. Handle: typo tolerance (edit distance), trending queries, and harmful query filtering.

problems.title: Design a Content Delivery Network (CDN)
problems.approach: DNS-based routing to nearest edge PoP. Edge servers cache content with LRU eviction. Cache hierarchy: edge -> regional -> origin shield -> origin. Purge API for cache invalidation (propagated via pub/sub). Handle: cache warming, stale-while-revalidate, and DDoS mitigation at edge.

problems.title: Design a Collaborative Document Editor (Google Docs)
problems.approach: OT with central server: clients send operations with revision number, server transforms concurrent ops. Persist operation log + periodic snapshots. WebSocket for real-time sync. Cursor positions as metadata operations. Offline support with operation queuing.
